intro: Baptisia cornigera, also known as yellow wild indigo or yellow indigo, is a perennial legume native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Baptisia cornigera include isoflavones, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
